desc: Implementation patterns @dev should default to. Quality-scored, anti-pattern aware. Seed nodes — extend with new q≥4 patterns after each significant slice.

nodes.title: spawn with array args over execSync template literals
nodes.s: When invoking external commands from Node, use spawn/execFile with an argv array. Never interpolate user-controlled values into a template-literal command string passed to execSync — that is shell-injection by construction.
nodes.not: execSync(`cmd ${userInput}`) — interpolation goes through /bin/sh and any whitespace/metachar in userInput becomes a new command
nodes.warn: applies even when input feels trusted — slugs, file paths, agent names can all contain shell metachars in tests/fixtures

nodes.title: git commit -m via HEREDOC for multi-line bodies
nodes.s: Multi-line commit messages must come from a HEREDOC with single-quoted EOF — anything else mangles backticks, $-escapes, and indentation.
nodes.p:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(scope): subject

Body paragraph.
EOF
)"
nodes.not: git commit -m "line1\nline2" — \n is literal; $vars expand; backticks execute
nodes.warn: the EOF marker must be single-quoted ('EOF') to prevent shell expansion inside the body

nodes.title: append-only updates to context cache files
nodes.s: Files that act as system state caches (bootstrap/current-state.md, project-pulse.md, features.md) are append-only. Never replace existing entries — add a new line/row. Other agents trust historical entries to remain stable.
nodes.p:
// good — Edit appending one bullet under existing section
// bad — Write replacing the whole file
nodes.not: regenerating the cache from scratch on every update — destroys the audit trail downstream agents read
nodes.warn: if an entry is wrong, mark it superseded with a follow-up entry — do not edit the original line

nodes.title: Read before Edit — surgical edits over rewrites
nodes.s: Always read the target file before editing. Prefer Edit (single old_string→new_string) over Write (full rewrite). Anchors must be unique enough to match exactly — surrounding context is your friend.
nodes.p:
// good — Edit with a 2-3 line unique anchor
// bad — Write the whole file just to change one line
nodes.src: Claude Code Edit tool contract; AIOSON dev.md execution invariants
nodes.not: Write to overwrite an existing file when only one section needs to change — loses concurrent edits and bypasses staleness checks
nodes.warn: if old_string isn't unique, EITHER widen the anchor OR use replace_all — never guess

nodes.title: small validated slices over big-bang implementation
nodes.s: Implement one slice → run the actual verification command (tsc, cargo check, npm test) → only then move to the next slice. Batched debugging at the end is the slowest path.
nodes.p: loop: { write code → run verification → fix → commit-ready }
nodes.src: dev.md Execution invariants; Motor AIOSON hardening rules
nodes.not: write 10 files then run tsc — error cascades make root cause hard to find
nodes.warn: verification command must be the real one (the one CI runs), not a proxy you trust
